title: Verify and fix Journey A characters appearing in Active Character dropdown
description: Journey A auto-saves character profiles to the DB during Generate Auditions (via createCharacter in auditionOrchestrator.js). The Active Character dropdown populates from listCharacters() called in initialLoad(). Journey A characters should therefore auto-appear — but this has not been verified end-to-end. If they don't appear (e.g. because initialLoad() runs before audition completes, or characters are filtered out), Journey A has no path to the Portfolio step. Fix: verify the full flow, and if needed refresh the character list after handleGenerateAudition completes. Update the guide to confirm characters auto-appear.
close_reason: listCharacters() called after handleGenerateAudition; savedCharacters state refreshed. First new character auto-selected in Active Character dropdown. Lines 328-340.

title: Fix Journey A ghost images: eliminate double-render from audition auto-queue + Portfolio step
description: Journey A auto-queues two Comfy jobs (front_portrait, profile_portrait) during Generate Auditions. These images land in the DB and appear in the Gallery. The convergence section then has Queue Portfolio which queues the same views again. A Journey A user who runs both steps ends up with two sets of images in the Gallery for the same character with no visual distinction. Decision required: (a) Journey A users skip the Portfolio queue step — their audition images are already in the Gallery, or (b) Journey A does not auto-queue Comfy jobs — it only generates the character profile, and the user queues images via the Portfolio step. Option (a) is the smaller change. Implement whichever is chosen and update the guide.
close_reason: Option (a): Journey A keeps auto-queue. Added: auto-select first generated character; informational note explaining front+profile are queued and how to avoid duplicates in Portfolio. Lines 558-563.

title: Fix More Takes gate: unlock panel on terminal Comfy state, not user approval
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, the More Takes panel is conditionally rendered only when result.views.some(v => auditionItemActions[v.auditionId]?.status === 'approved'). This means if both auto-queued Comfy jobs fail (bad output, ComfyUI error), the user can never approve a view and the More Takes panel never appears — they have no in-app path to request additional renders for a failed candidate. The gate should be: show More Takes when all views for a pair have reached a terminal Comfy status (success OR failed), regardless of approval state. Approval and requesting retakes are orthogonal actions.
close_reason: Gate changed to: show MoreTakesPanel when all views have reached terminal Comfy state (success or failed), not on user approval. Views without comfyPromptId count as terminal immediately. Lines 607-613.

title: Epic: Critical casting room flow fixes
description: Three flow-breaking bugs identified in casting_room_flow_analysis.md that will block real-world use: (1) Journey A auto-queues Comfy jobs AND the convergence section re-queues a Portfolio, producing ghost duplicate images with no distinction; (2) Journey A characters may not reliably surface in the Active Character dropdown; (3) More Takes panel is gated on user approval when it should be gated on job terminal state. These must ship before any other casting room work.
close_reason: All three critical flow bugs addressed in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx.

title: Phase 2: More Takes job tracking and auto-ingest
description: handleMoreTakes should store returned queued jobs in moreTakesState[characterId].jobs. After queueing, start the same auto-poll loop for those jobs. On success auto-ingest and append to auditionImages[characterId]. Show progress badge inside MoreTakesPanel.

title: Phase 1: Auto-poll + auto-ingest for audition jobs
description: After Generate Auditions succeeds, start an 8s interval that refreshes Comfy job statuses. When a job transitions to success, automatically ingest it and update auditionImages inline. Stop polling when all jobs are success or failed. Add per-view status badges (spinner / checkmark / error).

title: Debug: LM Studio audition timeout — fix JSON mode, max_tokens, thinking suppression
description:
User hits 'Local LM Studio request timed out' when pressing Generate Audition. Root causes identified:

1. **Thinking mode not properly suppressed**: lmStudioProvider sends /no_think as text prefix, but LM Studio 0.3+ supports enable_thinking=false API param for Qwen3 models. If the GGUF lacks the /no_think token, the model does full CoT reasoning and burns through max_tokens before outputting JSON — causing 120s timeout.

2. **max_tokens=2000 too low for audition**: 3 candidates × 15+ fields each = ~2500-3500 tokens of JSON. Model hits ceiling mid-output; LM Studio may hang or truncate silently.

3. **response_format not forwarded**: providerPayload carries responseFormat:'json' from auditionOrchestrator but lmStudioProvider ignores it. LM Studio supports response_format:{type:'json_object'} for constrained JSON output — without it the model may wrap output in markdown fences breaking parseJsonFromLlmText.

4. **No debug logging**: When timeout fires there is no trace of what was sent or how long it ran — impossible to diagnose further without adding request/response logging.

Fixes needed:
- Add enable_thinking:false to request body when LMSTUDIO_NO_THINK is true (LM Studio native suppression)
- Honor responseFormat:'json' by adding response_format:{type:'json_object'} to request body  
- Increase default max_tokens to 4000 (configurable via LMSTUDIO_MAX_TOKENS env var already exists)
- Add console.error/warn logging in lmStudioProvider for request start, response time, and errors

title: vercel.json: add maxDuration for character and batch API routes
description: vercel.json only sets maxDuration for api/polish.js (30s) and api/polish-health.js (10s). All other routes (characters.js, character-batch.js, characters-generate-batch.js, comfy-*.js, etc.) use Vercel's default timeout (10s on hobby / 60s on pro). Character generation and ComfyUI operations can exceed 10s, causing 504 timeouts in production. Fix: add maxDuration entries for the long-running routes.

title: Commit: App.jsx placeholder fixes + ErrorBoundary in main.jsx
description: Working tree has two uncommitted stability fixes: (1) App.jsx had  placeholder artifacts where toggleChip and mergeChips callbacks belong — they are implemented in the working tree but not committed. (2) main.jsx ErrorBoundary wraps App for runtime error visibility. Both are done; this task is just to verify, test, and commit them.

title: Fix: LM Studio provider leaks <think> blocks into output
description: Qwen3 models in LM Studio sometimes include <think>...</think> reasoning blocks in message.content even with /no_think prefix (e.g. older LM Studio builds or non-Qwen models that ignore the directive). The provider returns raw content without stripping these, so the polished prompt contains chain-of-thought noise. Fix: add a stripThinkBlocks() helper in shared.js and apply it to the parsed content before returning.

title: Fix: runPolish missing engine field in response
description: runPolish() returns {polished, provider, fallback} but usePolish.js reads data?.engine from the response — it's always null. resolveProviderSelection already tracks resolvedFrom (the engine); just add engine: providerSelection.resolvedFrom to the runPolish return value.

title: AI scene polish via /api/polish serverless endpoint
description: LLM-based prompt polishing via Claude (cloud), Ollama, LM Studio, and embedded (Tauri sidecar) providers. Shipped in api/polish.js, api/lib/polish/polishCore.js, and api/lib/llm/providers/.
close_reason: Already shipped — creating for historical accuracy

title: Vercel Production Config: create vercel.json and wire cloud deployment
description:
No vercel.json exists in the repo. The app targets Vercel but has no explicit routing/rewrite config, so serverless API routes (/api/*) may not resolve correctly in production.

What to do:
- Create vercel.json with rewrites routing /api/* to serverless functions
- Add build config (outputDirectory: dist, framework: vite)
- Reference ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
- Confirm build/output dirs match vite.config.js
- Document required env vars in README
close_reason: vercel.json created with APP_MODE=cloud, maxDuration for polish routes, and framework/build config. README deploy section updated with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY instructions.

title: Epic: Stability & Bug Fixes
description:
Address known reliability issues in providers, async flows, and debugging infrastructure.

Why: The polish pipeline has known fragility points (provider selection, fallback, metadata visibility) that affect real usage reliability.

How to apply: Bugs and reliability tasks belong here.

title: Epic: Infrastructure & Deployment
description:
Cloud deployment configuration, CI/CD wiring, and environment setup for production Vercel hosting.

Why: The app targets Vercel but has no vercel.json, so serverless API routes may not resolve in production. This epic tracks getting the app to a shippable hosted state.

How to apply: Infrastructure tasks (Vercel config, env wiring) belong here.

title: Stabilize LM Studio provider: /no_think, streaming, token budget
description: The lmStudioProvider has had three bugs patched this session (stream:false, max_tokens 400→2000, /no_think prefix for Qwen3 thinking models). Need to verify all fixes hold, write tests covering the key failure modes, and make the provider production-stable.
close_reason: Provider stabilized: /no_think patch, configurable max_tokens + no_think env vars, timeout fixed, 22 tests all green, full suite 210/210 pass

title: Mobile prompt output sticky bottom bar
description: On mobile (<960px), prompt output currently moves above controls which loses context. Need slim sticky bar at bottom showing prompt character count + 'View & Copy' button that opens modal with full prompt. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.3

title: Complete editable output field implementation
description: PARTIAL implementation exists. Current state: PromptOutput.jsx supports edit/restore + variant pinning + apply-diff flow. Missing: Full textarea auto-grow, complete sync prevention (chips → textarea only), robust reset functionality. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.1

title: Update CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md to reflect all flow and vocabulary fixes
description: After the critical flow fixes, vocabulary cleanup, and UX improvements land, the guide at docs/CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md needs to be updated to match. Key changes: (1) re-number steps within sections (not across sections); (2) remove redundant 'Save → Active Character' step if auto-promotion is implemented; (3) clarify which prerequisites apply to which journey; (4) update all action names to match renamed buttons; (5) add note about gallery being per-character; (6) remove the ghost-images confusion from Journey A convergence. This task should be the last to close in its dependency chain.

title: Add in-app batch generation form to Batch Pipeline section
description: The Batch Pipeline section currently only reviews pre-existing batches. Generating a batch requires calling POST /api/characters-generate-batch with persistBatch: true from outside the app. Non-technical users who see 'No batches found' have no in-app path forward. Fix: add a collapsible 'Generate Batch' form above the batch selector in the Batch Pipeline section. Minimum fields: age range (min/max), count (1-20), optional project tone, optional gender presentation. On submit, call the batch generation API with persistBatch: true, then refresh the batch list. Requires ENABLE_CHARACTER_BATCH_API=true — show a clear error/notice if the feature flag is off.

title: Resume Comfy polling on component re-mount for pending jobs
description: Audit and portfolio polling state lives in React component memory (auditPollRef, portfolioPollRef, auditionResults, portfolioJobs). If the user switches tabs while polling is active, the interval is cleared (or never called) and any subsequently completed Comfy jobs are not ingested. Users who navigate away and return will see 'pending' status forever and must use Developer Tools > Ingest Outputs manually — an undiscoverable recovery path. Fix: in useEffect on mount, check for non-terminal jobs (call getComfyJobsStatus for any pending auditionResults / portfolioJobs) and resume the appropriate poll. This requires persisting enough job state to survive a re-render (consider sessionStorage or an API call to re-fetch job lists).

title: Expose workflow selector in Journey A Generate Auditions step
description: Journey A auto-queues Comfy jobs during Generate Auditions, but the workflow used is never surfaced to the user. The Portfolio section has a workflow selector but by then Journey A users may not reach it (see ghost images issue). A user with a specific ComfyUI workflow cannot specify it for the audition auto-queue. Fix: add the workflow selector dropdown to the Cast from Bank section, immediately above or beside the Generate Auditions button. Reuse the same selectableWorkflows state that already exists. The selected workflow should be passed as providerPayload to the audition API.

title: Auto-load prompt packs when Active Character is selected
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, the Active Character section has two buttons: 'Compile Prompt Packs' and 'Load Existing'. The user must remember whether they compiled packs in a previous session. This is a developer-facing decision leaking into UX. Fix: on selectedCharacterId change, automatically call listPromptPacksForCharacter. If packs exist, show them immediately. Expose 'Recompile' as a secondary action. If no packs exist, show 'Compile Prompt Packs' as the single primary CTA. Remove the 'Load Existing' button — loading should be automatic.

title: Rename batch system classification labels to avoid overlap with reviewStatus
description: In the Batch Pipeline candidate cards, both 'classification' (system, similarity-based: accepted/needsMutation/rejected) and 'reviewStatus' (user: pending/approved/rejected) are displayed. The word 'rejected' appears in both with different meanings. Rename display labels for the system classification field to non-overlapping vocabulary: 'unique' (was accepted), 'needs change' (was needsMutation), 'too similar' (was rejected). The underlying DB values do not need to change — only the display strings in CastingPipelinePanel.jsx.

title: Rename 'Character Bank Entry' input concept to 'Casting Brief'
description: The Cast from Bank section uses a 'Character Bank Entry' — a text brief (name + description) used as LLM seed input. This collides with 'Actor Bank' / 'Character Bank' vocabulary used to mean the collection of finished output characters. These are opposite ends of the pipeline (input seed vs output character) with nearly identical names. Rename the input concept throughout: UI labels, empty states, section headers. Suggestion: 'Casting Brief', 'Role Brief', or 'Character Brief'. Reserve 'Bank' vocabulary for finished/output characters only.

title: Rename gallery Approve/Reject to 'Keep' / 'Discard'
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx Gallery section, the Approve/Reject buttons call approveGeneratedImage/rejectGeneratedImage — they mark a specific GENERATED IMAGE as approved. This is the third distinct meaning of 'Approve' in the same UI. Rename to 'Keep' / 'Discard' (or 'Shortlist' / 'Discard') to make clear this is image curation, not character or view approval.

title: Rename batch candidate Approve button to 'Cast this character'
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx Batch Pipeline section, the Approve button calls approveBatchCandidate — it marks a CHARACTER PROFILE as approved (no images involved at this stage). Using 'Approve' here collides with the gallery image and audition view approve actions. Rename to something that conveys 'I want to proceed with this character profile': suggestion 'Cast this character' or 'Use profile'. The Reject button can stay 'Reject' or become 'Dismiss'.

title: Rename audition view Approve/Reject buttons to 'Select this look' / 'Pass'
description: In CastingPipelinePanel.jsx, the Approve/Reject buttons on each audition view card call approveActorAudition/rejectActorAudition — they mark a VIEW RECORD as approved, not a character or image. Using the same label 'Approve' as the batch candidate card and gallery card creates a three-way collision. Rename to something that conveys 'I like this specific rendered look': suggestions 'Select' / 'Pass' or 'Keep this look' / 'Skip'. Update button labels in JSX and any ARIA labels.

title: Epic: Journey B in-app batch generation
description: Journey B (Batch Pipeline) currently requires hitting a raw API endpoint (POST /api/characters-generate-batch with persistBatch: true) before the UI can be used at all. There is no in-app trigger. The empty state message 'No batches found' gives users no path forward. Either add a Generate Batch form to the Batch Pipeline section, or label Journey B as developer-only and remove it from the user-facing guide.

title: Epic: Casting room UX improvements
description: Four UX friction points from casting_room_flow_analysis.md: (1) Compile vs Load Existing prompt packs requires user to remember previous session state — should auto-load on character select; (2) Journey A auto-queues with an undocumented default workflow, no way to choose; (3) Gallery has no visible label showing which character it belongs to; (4) Comfy polling is lost on tab navigation — jobs may complete but never be ingested unless user knows to use Developer Tools manually.

title: Epic: Approval UX and vocabulary cleanup
description: The word 'Approve' is used for three different objects (audition view record, batch candidate profile, generated image) with three different effects. Additionally, 'Character Bank Entry' collides with 'Actor Bank' naming, and system classification labels ('rejected', 'accepted') overlap with user review status labels. This creates an inconsistent mental model. Rename each action and field to be semantically distinct.

title: Write Casting Room how-to guide
description: Create a step-by-step user guide for the Casting Room workflow. Cover both journeys: Cast from Bank (audition flow) and Batch Pipeline (batch→portfolio flow). Verify each step against the actual code. Guide should be accurate, numbered, and cover the full end-to-end process including setup prerequisites.
close_reason: How-to guide written at docs/CASTING_ROOM_HOWTO.md covering both journeys, prerequisites, troubleshooting. Verified against actual component code.

title: Phase 4: Casting Room layout restructure
description: Reorder sections to match actual workflow: 1) Cast from Bank, 2) Batch Pipeline (merge Batches+Candidates), 3) Active Character, 4) Portfolio (auto poll/ingest), 5) Gallery, 6) Developer Tools collapsed. Merge Batches and Candidates into one Batch Pipeline section where selecting a batch inline-expands candidates.

title: Phase 3: UX friction reduction in Casting Room
description: A) Merge Compile+List prompt packs into one action. B) Remove Build Portfolio Plan as a separate user step - absorb into Queue Portfolio. C) Show character name+age in Saved Character dropdown instead of UUID. D) Show character name prominently in candidate list. E) Move Job+Ingest section behind a Developer Tools collapsible.

title: P2: ActorBankView — filters, grid, and ActorCard component
description: Build the Actor Bank view: ActorBankView.jsx (top-level, fetches /api/characters, manages filter state), ActorBankFilters.jsx (search input + gender chips + age range, live 300ms debounce), ActorBankGrid.jsx (CSS grid with loading/empty states), ActorCard.jsx (200x280 card with thumbnail, name, age/gender, archetype). Full-width layout, CSS Modules, existing custom properties only.

title: P2: NavBar component + currentView state in App.jsx
description: Add a minimal top-level navigation bar (max 48px height) with two tabs: Prompt Builder and Actor Bank. Add currentView state to App.jsx (values: promptBuilder | actorBank). Conditionally render views. Prompt builder state must persist across view switches. Use only existing CSS custom properties from index.css.

title: P2: Enhance GET /api/characters with search and filter params
description: listCharacters() only supports projectId and embeddingStatus filters. The Actor Bank UI needs search (name/cinematicArchetype text match), gender (genderPresentation field), ageMin/ageMax (age field). Since characters are stored as JSON blobs, add JS-level filtering in listCharacters. Update both vite.config.js middleware and api/characters.js handler to pass these params.

title: Snake_case @token slugs with backward-compatible lookup
description: Reusable slugify utility: toSnakeSlug, withUniqueSuffix, resolveCharacterSlug in src/utils/slugify.js. CharacterBuilder.jsx consumes them for save flow; assembler.js uses resolveCharacterSlug for @token expansion with kebab/snake backward compat.
close_reason: Already shipped — creating for historical accuracy

title: Rename tabs: Pipeline -> Casting Room, Characters -> Character Builder
description: UI copy-only change to align tab labels with intended product names. Shipped in commits 97ce821 and 8b8c6b4. Tab state keys and component names are unchanged by design.
close_reason: Already shipped — creating for historical accuracy

title: ComfyUI image generation pipeline
description: Full API route set for ComfyUI queue, status, ingest, and workflow management. Shipped in api/lib/comfy/comfyService.js and registered in vite.config.js. Workflows in api/lib/comfy/workflows/.
close_reason: Already shipped — creating for historical accuracy

title: Casting Room Phase 3: DB-backed character bank persistence
description:
Character Builder currently persists to localStorage (qpb_characters_v1). No create/update endpoints for user-authored characters exist yet — Phase 3 of the Casting Room plan.

What to implement (see docs/CASTING_ROOM_PLAN.md Phase 3):
- Add slug/description/tag fields to characters table in api/lib/db/schema.js
- Create POST /api/character-create and PUT /api/character-update endpoints in vite.config.js
- Update CharacterBuilder.jsx to write to DB via new endpoints
- Migrate existing localStorage characters on first launch

Blocks: Phases 4-6 of Casting Room plan.
close_reason: Auto-sync on save: saveCharacter() now triggers syncCharacter() in background after writing to localStorage. Startup merge: bank entries absent from localStorage are pulled in on mount (DB wins for missing slugs). Both paths degrade gracefully when bank is unreachable.

title: Prompt history and session memory (localStorage)
description: Store last N prompts in localStorage with undo/redo support. Workspace profiles stored in qpb_workspace_profiles_v1. Shipped in src/hooks/useWorkspaceHistory.js.
close_reason: Already shipped — creating for historical accuracy

title: Reference Image AI Analysis: backend routes for visual feature extraction
description:
No backend route exists for analyzing uploaded reference images. The image reference upload (visual palette) is UI-only; the roadmap calls for actual visual feature extraction.

What to implement:
- POST /api/analyze-reference-image — accepts image data URL, returns extracted visual features (palette, mood, lighting style, composition notes)
- Wire to a vision-capable LLM provider (Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 supports vision input)
- Return structured data usable to pre-populate chip selections or style notes in frontend
- Add frontend wiring in PromptOutput or a new ReferenceBoard component
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/analyze-reference-image using Claude vision. Returns structured features (palette, lighting, composition, film, mood, chipSuggestions). ReferenceBoard.jsx updated with Analyze button and inline results display.

title: Epic: UX Augmentations
description:
Surface-level feature additions and improvements to the main builder experience.

Why: Several planned features from the design audit are ready to build now that core plumbing is stable.

How to apply: New UI features and prompt-quality improvements belong here.

title: Main-UI image gallery: mirror generated images into builder tab
description: Users can't see generated images without switching to the Casting Pipeline tab. Add a collapsible 'Generated images' panel to PromptOutput that fetches from /api/generated-images, shows thumbnails in a grid, and lets users click through to full-size. Mirrors the existing history/saved-prompts toggle pattern so it feels native to the output panel.
close_reason: Implemented: collapsible gallery panel in PromptOutput, 4-col grid, lazy thumbnails, click-to-fullsize, refresh button, zero polling cost

title: Expand REWRITES: body/posture language
description: The REWRITES table now covers locations, weather, and garments. The next gap is body/posture language — phrases users naturally type that should expand into cinematically specific physical descriptions. Examples to add: 'sitting' -> 'seated, weight forward, elbows on knees', 'standing' -> 'standing still, weight on one foot, not at attention', 'lying down' -> 'lying on one side, not quite asleep', 'walking' -> 'walking at unhurried pace, no apparent destination', 'leaning' -> 'leaning against the wall, shoulder into it'. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md §3.3 and constants.js REWRITES for the pattern.

title: Garment/clothing expander in scene rewriting
description: Current REWRITES table handles locations and weather but not clothing. Add rewrite rules for common garment terms with material-specific language: gray raincoat → 'gray wool raincoat, collar turned up, dark with moisture at shoulders', overcoat → 'heavy wool overcoat, worn at elbows', suit → 'dark suit, not recently pressed', etc. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.7

title: Time of day / weather quick-set buttons
description: Small button row (not chips) that pre-selects combination of light + texture + color chips for common conditions: Dawn, Morning overcast, Midday harsh, Golden hour, Blue hour, Night rain, Night clear, Fog. Should merge with (not replace) existing chip selections. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.6

title: Composition modifiers panel
description: Add new chip section '06 — Composition' between Environment and Light. Current gap: nothing specifies WHERE in frame subjects are placed. Include chips for: figures at left/right third, centered symmetrical, figures small/low in frame, background placement, blurred foreground, high/low angle, silhouette. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.5

title: Section open/close persistence
description: All collapsible sections reset to closed on page load. Save open/closed state to localStorage with key qpb_sections. Each section has an id - use that as the key. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.4

title: Add character rename and archive actions to Active Character section
description: The Active Character dropdown grows indefinitely with no management options. After several sessions the list becomes unmanageable. Minimum viable character management: (1) rename — inline edit for the character name field, saved via API; (2) archive — soft-remove from Active Character dropdown without deleting from DB (set a flag or filter). These are the building blocks the Actor Bank UI brief describes. The archive action should move the character to a collapsible 'Archived' section rather than hard delete, since generated images reference the character ID.

title: Add 'Showing images for: [Name]' label to Gallery section header
description: The Gallery section displays images for whichever character is currently selected in the Active Character dropdown. Switching characters silently changes the gallery contents. A user who generated images for character A, then switches to B, may think they lost A's images. Fix: add a visible 'Showing images for: [Character Name]' label in the Gallery section header, directly below the section title. This makes clear the gallery is a filtered per-character view, not a global collection. Use the savedCharacters array to resolve name from selectedCharacterId.

title: Epic: Character management (rename and archive)
description: The Active Character dropdown accumulates characters indefinitely with no way to rename or archive them. After generating images for several characters across sessions the dropdown becomes unmanageable. Minimum viable management: rename and archive (soft-delete from dropdown). This is the foundation the Actor Bank UI from the implementation brief would build on.

title: P6: Tests for actor-more-takes handler (actorCandidateId lookup path)
description: Add unit tests for api/actor-more-takes.js covering: (1) happy path with characterId directly, (2) lookup via actorCandidateId resolving notes JSON to characterId, (3) missing candidate returns 404, (4) missing both characterId and actorCandidateId returns 400. Mock queueCharacterPortfolio and the db.

title: P6: Tests for listCharacters filter enhancements (search, gender, age)
description: Add test coverage in api/lib/db/db.test.js for the new listCharacters filter params added in P2: search (name/cinematicArchetype), gender (genderPresentation substring), ageMin, ageMax. Tests should verify each filter in isolation and combined. Use existing db.test.js createTempDb helper pattern.

title: P3-Ph5: CastingPipelinePanel — more takes UI for approved actor candidates
description: Add a 'More Takes' section in CastingPipelinePanel that appears after an actor candidate has been approved AND saved (has a characterId). Shows view type checkboxes (front_portrait, three_quarter_portrait, profile_portrait, full_body, audition_still, cinematic_scene) and a Queue Takes button. Posts to /api/actor-more-takes. Shows queued job count feedback.

title: P3-Ph5: POST /api/actor-more-takes — queue additional views for approved candidate
description: After an actor candidate is approved and saved as a character, users need to queue more ComfyUI views. New endpoint POST /api/actor-more-takes accepts {characterId, views, workflowId?} and delegates to queueCharacterPortfolio (already exists in api/lib/portfolio/characterPortfolio.js). Also register the route in vite.config.js dev middleware.

title: Export prompt as .txt file download
description: Download button saves prompt as qpb-[director]-[timestamp].txt with negative prompt on second line. Shipped in src/utils/downloadPromptFile.js, used by PromptOutput.jsx.

title: Prompt diff view: assembled vs manually edited

title: Casting Room Phase 6: tests and docs hardening for Character + Actor + Casting Room flows
description:
Phase 6 of the Casting Room plan. Lock behavior with tests and docs for the Character bank, Actor bank, and Casting Room flows once Phases 3-5 ship.

Scope:

Phase 5 of the Casting Room plan. After an actor candidate is approved, users need a path to generate additional views/poses bound to that actor's identity.

Builds on existing mutate/refill primitives (vite.config.js:L347-L437) and portfolio queue.

Phase 4 of the Casting Room plan (docs/CASTING_ROOM_PLAN.md). Currently the system supports arbitrary view arrays. Phase 4 defines an audition unit as a mandatory front_portrait + profile_portrait pair per generation attempt.

Files to change:

- src/components/CastingPipelinePanel.jsx (render as pair unit)

Depends on Phase 3 completion.
close_reason: auditionOrchestrator now generates front+side pairs per character. views param replaces single view. pairId/view stored in candidate notes. UI removes view dropdown, renders results as paired columns. Tests updated.

title: Director grid: fix overflow with 60 directors
description: The director grid uses flex-wrap which produces uneven chip widths at 60 directors. Switch to CSS grid with auto-fill and minmax(80px, 1fr) columns so the grid fills evenly at any panel width. Custom director slots (added in v2a) must continue to appear after a visual separator. Affects DirectorSection.module.css .dirGrid rule only — no logic changes needed.

title: Saved prompts panel in output area
description: Workspace profiles (qpb_workspace_profiles_v1) cover full-state save/restore but there is no quick 'Save prompt' action on the output panel itself. Add a dedicated Save button to PromptOutput that saves the current prompt text (assembled or polished), director key, scene, and timestamp under a user-given name. Display saved entries in a collapsible panel on the right. Allow rename and delete. This is the 'explicit Save with name' piece missing from the PARTIAL 7.9 item in AGENT_HANDOFF.md.
close_reason: Saved prompts panel added to PromptOutput: Save button in header, collapsible list with copy/restore/rename/delete per entry, persisted to localStorage under qpb_saved_prompts_v1, max 30 entries. Also fixed pre-existing ChipSection  syntax corruption from earlier session.

title: Image reference upload (visual palette)
description: Optional image upload on right panel displayed as small thumbnail. UX hint feature only (not connected to API) - helps users keep visual reference in view while building prompt. No processing needed. Store as data URL in state (not localStorage - too large). See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.13
close_reason: Already implemented: ReferenceBoard component (src/components/ReferenceBoard.jsx) is fully built and wired into the right panel at App.jsx:887. Supports up to 8 images, thumbnails, notes, remove. Uses createObjectURL (in-memory state, not localStorage) matching the spec intent.

title: Custom director slots (power user feature)
description: Allow users to create 2-3 custom director entries. Each requires: name, short name, note, and 3-4 scenario templates per character count. Store in localStorage. Render in director grid after built-in directors. Put behind 'Advanced' toggle or separate 'Custom' section. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md section 7.10

value: Qwen Prompt Builder (Prompt-o-matic): Cinematic prompt assembly tool for Qwen image generation. Stack: Vite+React, Node API, optional Tauri desktop. 60 directors with interaction scenarios. Features AI polish via Claude/Ollama/LM Studio, character builder, casting pipeline, ComfyUI integration. Key files: App.jsx (state), assembler.js (prompt assembly), polishCore.js (LLM integration). Architecture is 3-layer: React frontend, Node API, domain logic core. See AGENT_HANDOFF.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md, .cursorrules for detailed context.
